content:
Case 5:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 56
- Sex: female
- Chief complaint: Progressive fatigue, pallor, exertional dyspnea, and spontaneous bruising with petechiae
- Vital signs: BP 124/78 mmHg, HR 99/min, T° 37.1 °C, RR 16/min, SpO₂ 96% on room air.
Setting: Inpatient admission for newly detected blasts and cytopenias
Capabilities: You are evaluating a hemodynamically stable patient with suspected acute hematologic malignancy in a hospital setting. You can perform a focused history and examination, obtain routine laboratory studies, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging appropriate for an initial inpatient assessment. Invasive diagnostic procedures and complex analyses require additional coordination but can be planned from this setting.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "complete blood count with differential and peripheral blood smear"}}

response.request: complete blood count with differential and peripheral blood smear

content:
Cbc:
CBC: Hb 7.9 g/dL, WBC 13.1 ×10⁹/L, Plt 18 ×10⁹/L; differential: 78% blasts, 10% monocytes, 8% neutrophils, 4% lymphocytes.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "peripheral blood smear examination"}}
'cbc' already provided.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "coagulation studies (PT, aPTT, fibrinogen)"}}

Coagulation:
Coagulation: aPTT 38 sec, INR 1.7.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "blood smear morphology report (description of blasts, Auer rods, granules)"}}

response.request: blood smear morphology report (description of blasts, Auer rods, granules)

content:
'blood smear morphology report (description of blasts, Auer rods, granules)' not available. Try 'cbc' instead.
This case does not include a dedicated peripheral blood smear morphology report item. A complete blood count with differential is available in this round and may provide related information about circulating blasts and differential counts.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "fibrinogen level"}}
'coagulation' already provided.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone marrow aspiration and biopsy results"}}

response.request: bone marrow aspiration and biopsy results

content:
'bone_marrow' available in round 2.
The case includes bone marrow aspiration/biopsy results, but those are not available in this round. Consider requesting labs or imaging available in round 1 (for example CBC, chemistry, or relevant imaging).

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Acute promyelocytic leukemia (APL) with coagulopathy likely DIC", "differentials": ["Other subtypes of acute myeloid leukemia (AML)", "Acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL)", "Myelodysplastic syndrome with excess blasts (MDS-EB)"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Administer all-trans retinoic acid (ATRA) immediately", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Platelet transfusion to maintain platelets > 50 \u00d710\u2079/L", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Fresh frozen plasma and cryoprecipitate as needed for coagulopathy (target fibrinogen > 150 mg/dL)", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and molecular testing (including PML-RARA)", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate antibiotics for febrile neutropenia prophylaxis if needed", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Consider addition of idarubicin once APL confirmed", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Acute promyelocytic leukemia (APL) with coagulopathy likely DIC
differentials: Other subtypes of acute myeloid leukemia (AML)

treatment_plan.action: Fresh frozen plasma and cryoprecipitate as needed for coagulopathy (target fibrinogen > 150 mg/dL)
Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and molecular testing (including PML-RARA)
Initiate antibiotics for febrile neutropenia prophylaxis if needed
Consider addition of idarubicin once APL confirmed

content: {"evaluation": {"working_diagnosis": "partially_correct", "differentials": ["partially_correct", "correct", "incorrect"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "correct", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "partially_correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}]}}

Round 2 begins.
Setting: Inpatient Ward (Definitive Hematologic Workup)
Capabilities: The patient remains admitted for clarification of a suspected acute leukemia. You can organize bone marrow assessment, extended diagnostic workup, and baseline staging procedures. Interdisciplinary evaluations can be requested to exclude alternative primary malignancies or additional disease sites. Some specialized tests may not yet be available.
Request additional information or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone marrow aspiration and biopsy results including morphology, blast percentage, flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and molecular studies"}}

response.request: bone marrow aspiration and biopsy results including morphology, blast percentage, flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and molecular studies

content:
Bone Marrow:
Morphology: Hypocellular bone marrow (20%) with approximately 80% infiltration by round to oval-shaped blasts showing open chromatin, prominent nucleoli, and deeply basophilic cytoplasm. Dysplastic megakaryocytes and granulopoiesis were noted. Residual hematopoiesis was markedly reduced with impaired erythropoiesis. No excess of plasma cells was seen. Flow cytometry: Blasts were positive for CD34, CD117, HLA-DR, CD13, and CD33, with aberrant expression of CD56. CD7 was negative. Plasma cells were polyclonal and accounted for <1% of total nucleated cells.
Immunohistochemistry confirmed hypocellularity of the marrow. Reticulin staining demonstrated fibrosis grade MF-2. Infiltration by CD34+ blasts (70%) with co-expression of CD117 and CD33 was confirmed. No atypical or monoclonal plasma cell population was identified.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Cytogenetic analysis (karyotype), FISH for PML-RARA, and molecular mutation panel (FLT3, NPM1, CEBPA)"}}

response.request: Cytogenetic analysis (karyotype), FISH for PML-RARA, and molecular mutation panel (FLT3, NPM1, CEBPA)

content:
Cytogenetics:
Preliminary cytogenetics
47,XX,+1,del(17p13.1),-20,dup(1q)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "fibrinogen and D-dimer"}}
'coagulation' already provided.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Acute myeloid leukemia with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC), adverse-risk (complex karyotype, del17p)", "differentials": ["Acute promyelocytic leukemia", "Therapy-related myeloid neoplasm", "Myelodysplastic syndrome with excess blasts"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "FISH for PML-RARA to definitively rule out APL", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Send molecular testing for FLT3, NPM1, CEBPA, TP53, IDH1/2, and ASXL1 for risk stratification and targeted therapy options", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Echocardiogram to assess left ventricular ejection fraction prior to anthracycline", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Induction chemotherapy with 7+3 (cytarabine continuous infusion for 7 days and daunorubicin 60-90 mg/m2 for 3 days) after excluding APL and confirming cardiac function, with consideration of adding midostaurin if FLT3 mutation positive", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Allopurinol and intravenous hydration for tumor lysis prophylaxis", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Platelet transfusion to maintain platelet count >10-20 \u00d710\u2079/L or for active bleeding", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Packed red blood cell transfusion for symptomatic anemia or hemoglobin <8 g/dL", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Fresh frozen plasma and cryoprecipitate as needed for coagulopathy (target INR <1.5, fibrinogen >150 mg/dL)", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate antimicrobial prophylaxis with levofloxacin, posaconazole, and acyclovir upon development of neutropenia", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Acute myeloid leukemia with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC), adverse-risk (complex karyotype, del17p)

treatment_plan.action: FISH for PML-RARA to definitively rule out APL
Send molecular testing for FLT3, NPM1, CEBPA, TP53, IDH1/2, and ASXL1 for risk stratification and targeted therapy options
Echocardiogram to assess left ventricular ejection fraction prior to anthracycline

Induction chemotherapy with 7+3 (cytarabine continuous infusion for 7 days and daunorubicin 60-90 mg/m2 for 3 days) after excluding APL and confirming cardiac function, with consideration of adding midostaurin if FLT3 mutation positive
Allopurinol and intravenous hydration for tumor lysis prophylaxis
Platelet transfusion to maintain platelet count >10-20 ×10⁹/L or for active bleeding
Packed red blood cell transfusion for symptomatic anemia or hemoglobin <8 g/dL
Fresh frozen plasma and cryoprecipitate as needed for coagulopathy (target INR <1.5, fibrinogen >150 mg/dL)
Initiate antimicrobial prophylaxis with levofloxacin, posaconazole, and acyclovir upon development of neutropenia

content: {"evaluation": {"working_diagnosis": "partially_correct", "differentials": ["incorrect", "correct", "correct"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "incorrect", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "partially_correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}]}}

Round 3 begins.
Setting: Inpatient Ward / Pre-transplant Treatment Strategy Discussion
Capabilities: You now have access to results from specialized tests with longer turnaround times, including molecular profiling, detailed classification of the leukemia, and a comprehensive treatment history. You are expected to integrate these data with risk scores and transplant information to define prognosis and formulate a definitive treatment and transplant strategy.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "AML next-generation sequencing mutation panel and FISH results for common AML rearrangements (including PML-RARA, RUNX1-RUNX1T1, CBFB-MYH11, KMT2A)"}}

response.request: AML next-generation sequencing mutation panel and FISH results for common AML rearrangements (including PML-RARA, RUNX1-RUNX1T1, CBFB-MYH11, KMT2A)

content:
Molecular Panel:
CD138- fraction: Mutations in PTPN11 (VAF 11%), TP53 p.R248Q (VAF 28%); TP53 p.Y220C (VAF 12%). Negative for ASXL1, RUNX1, BCOR, CEBPA, DDX41, FLT3, IDH1/2, NPM1, SRSF2, STAG2, U2AF1, ZRSR2, KMT2A or MECOM rearrangements

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Acute myeloid leukemia with mutated TP53 (adverse risk) and complex karyotype, consistent with AML with TP53 mutation (WHO 2022)", "differentials": ["AML with myelodysplasia-related changes", "Therapy-related myeloid neoplasm", "Myelodysplastic syndrome with excess blasts-2 (MDS-EB2)"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Venetoclax + azacitidine induction (azacitidine 75 mg/m2 days 1-7, venetoclax ramp up to 400 mg daily days 1-28) with tumor lysis prophylaxis (allopurinol, hydration, monitoring)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Transfusion support: maintain platelet count >10 \u00d710^9/L (or >20 if bleeding/fever), maintain hemoglobin >8 g/dL, correct coagulopathy with FFP/cryoprecipitate as needed", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Antimicrobial prophylaxis: levofloxacin 500 mg daily (when neutropenic), posaconazole 300 mg daily (or alternative antifungal), acyclovir 400 mg twice daily", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "HLA typing and initiate unrelated donor search; evaluate for matched related donors", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Discuss and offer enrollment in clinical trials for TP53-mutated AML", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Bone marrow biopsy after cycle 1 to assess response and minimal residual disease (flow cytometry, molecular for TP53)", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Echocardiogram and pulmonary function tests for transplant evaluation", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "If complete remission achieved, proceed to allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation after appropriate consolidation", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Acute myeloid leukemia with mutated TP53 (adverse risk) and complex karyotype, consistent with AML with TP53 mutation (WHO 2022)

treatment_plan.action: Venetoclax + azacitidine induction (azacitidine 75 mg/m2 days 1-7, venetoclax ramp up to 400 mg daily days 1-28) with tumor lysis prophylaxis (allopurinol, hydration, monitoring)
Transfusion support: maintain platelet count >10 ×10^9/L (or >20 if bleeding/fever), maintain hemoglobin >8 g/dL, correct coagulopathy with FFP/cryoprecipitate as needed
Antimicrobial prophylaxis: levofloxacin 500 mg daily (when neutropenic), posaconazole 300 mg daily (or alternative antifungal), acyclovir 400 mg twice daily
HLA typing and initiate unrelated donor search; evaluate for matched related donors
Discuss and offer enrollment in clinical trials for TP53-mutated AML
Bone marrow biopsy after cycle 1 to assess response and minimal residual disease (flow cytometry, molecular for TP53)
Echocardiogram and pulmonary function tests for transplant evaluation
If complete remission achieved, proceed to allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation after appropriate consolidation
